questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 5, 15, 45, 135, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows a Geometric Progression with a constant multiplier ratio of 3. So, 135 * 3 = 405.
questions.trick: If numbers scale up very quickly, look for multiplication rather than addition.

questions.question: A can complete a piece of work in 38 days while B can complete the same work in 32 days. If they work together, in how many days will they complete the work?
questions.explanation: Work = 38 * 32 = 1216. Rate = 38 + 32 = 70. Time = Work / Rate = 17.37 days.

questions.question: Which is the largest planet in our solar system?
What is the average of the following 5 consecutive even numbers: 52, 54, 56, 58, 60?
questions.trick: For a consecutive arithmetic sequence, the average is simply the exact middle term.

questions.question: Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 4, 9, 16, 50
questions.explanation: 4, 9, and 16 are perfect squares. 50 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.

questions.question: A train travels at an average speed of 88 km/hr. How much distance will it cover in 6 hours?
questions.trick: Basic multiplication rule: D = S * T.

questions.question: Who wrote the national anthem of India?
If an article is bought for Rs. 920 and sold at a profit of 20%, what is the selling price?
questions.trick: Calculate 10% of CP, multiply, and add it conceptually.

questions.question: If BAT is coded as CBU, how will CUP be coded in that same language?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by exactly 1 positions in the English alphabet.
questions.trick: Note the position gap between the first letter of the word and the first letter of its code.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
Find the next number in the series: 14, 20, 26, 32, 38, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows an Arithmetic Progression with a constant difference of 6. So, 38 + (6) = 44.
questions.trick: Always check the difference between the first and second terms and verify across the series.
